Title: Characterization of SrUGT76G4 reveals a key residue for regioselectivity and efficient Reb M synthesis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Stevia rebaudiana; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Steviol glycoside; diterpenoid glycoside; noncaloric sweetener
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-intensity natural sweetener with sucrose-like sweetness and lack of bitter/off-tastes
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late glycosylation steps converting Reb E/Reb D toward Reb M; C19 glycosylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT); PSPG box-containing GT-B fold glucosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified in high-Reb D/Reb M cultivars HDM2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently active in Nicotiana benthamiana; heterologously expressed in Escherichia coli BL21 (DE3) for biochemical assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; optimal activity at pH 8.25 and 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Reb D; Reb E; also STV, Rub, Reb A
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reb M from Reb D; Reb M and Reb AM from Reb E; Reb A/Reb I/Reb E2 from STV; STV B from Rub
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to steviol glycosides through β-1,3 glucosylation, with strong preference for glycosylation at the C19 position, efficiently converting Reb D to Reb M and Reb E to Reb M/Reb AM
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Proposed key enzyme responsible for Reb M biosynthesis in Stevia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes efficient Reb M synthesis and enables rare steviol glycoside formation through C19-selective glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; HPLC and high-resolution MS/MS product profiling; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; enzyme kinetics; crystal structures; mutagenesis; molecular dynamics simulations; fed-batch biotransformation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT76G4 was identified as a natural UGT76G1 variant from high-Reb D/Reb M Stevia cultivars. 2. It is the first reported β-1,3 glucosyltransferase with C19 regioselectivity on steviol glycosides. 3. It efficiently converts Reb D to Reb M and preferentially converts Reb E toward Reb M/Reb AM rather than Reb D. 4. Structural, mutational, and MD analyses showed residue 200 is pivotal for regioselectivity. 5. Engineered variants based on SrUGT76G4 further improved Reb M production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomic study of Stevia cultivars referenced from prior work; no new accession reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reb E and Reb D have low aqueous solubility in fed-batch reactions; possible product inhibition and enzyme inactivation during prolonged conversion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for metabolic engineering and biocatalytic production of Reb M; useful for engineering Stevia or microbial platforms to increase next-generation sweetener yield; serves as template for rational enzyme engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from UGT76G1 by strong C19 regioselectivity; also generates rare products including STV B, Reb E2, and Reb AM. Concluded to be the key native enzyme for Reb M biosynthesis in Stevia.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Stevia rebaudiana; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Steviol glycoside; diterpenoid glycoside; noncaloric sweetener
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural sweetener biosynthesis intermediate/product affecting sweetness quality
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late glycosylation steps at C13/C19 positions of steviol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT); PSPG box-containing GT-B fold glucosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected exclusively in high-Reb A cultivar HRA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana; heterologously expressed in Escherichia coli BL21 (DE3) for biochemical assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; optimal activity at pH 7.25 and 40 °C
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reb A from STV; Reb D from Reb E; limited Reb M from Reb E or Reb D
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to steviol glycosides via β-1,3 glycosylation, showing predominant C13 regioselectivity and limited C19 activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor and can also directly form target product at low efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Known pivotal pathway enzyme, but not effective enough for enhancing Reb M in this study context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predominantly directs flux toward Reb A and Reb D rather than efficient Reb M accumulation due to C13 preference
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative enzyme assays; HPLC/MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; kinetics; structural comparison; mutagenesis/chimeric swaps relative to SrUGT76G4
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT76G1 served as the benchmark steviol glycosyltransferase for comparison with SrUGT76G4. 2. It preferentially glycosylates the C13 position and mainly converts Reb E to Reb D rather than Reb M. 3. Its catalytic efficiency toward Reb D-to-Reb M conversion is much lower than that of SrUGT76G4. 4. Residue L200 alone is insufficient to switch its regioselectivity, indicating broader sequence context controls activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression previously failed to improve Reb M levels in Stevia; lower efficiency for C19-dependent Reb M synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparison template and engineering scaffold, but less suitable than SrUGT76G4-derived variants for industrial Reb M production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background-established pathway enzyme but also directly assayed throughout Results as a principal comparator; accession ACM47734.1 belongs to UGT76G2, not SrUGT76G1.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Steviol glycoside; diterpenoid glycoside; noncaloric sweetener
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sweetener intermediate relevant to palatable steviol glycoside production
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative glycosylation of Rub toward Reb E; β-1,2 glucosylation branch with C19 preference
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed in vitro with Rub, STV, and Reb A
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays; comparison of wild type and L204 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Wild type primarily forms Rub X from Rub; L204G primarily forms STV and suggests an alternative route to Reb E
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes β-1,2 glucosylation of steviol glycosides with native C19 preference; mutation at the residue equivalent to position 200 changes product regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate/alternative precursor to target sweeteners
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: L204G shifts regioselectivity toward C13 and suggests an alternative pathway for more efficient Reb E synthesis from Rub
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis and in vitro product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsUGT91C1 was used to test whether the residue equivalent to SrUGT76G4 position 200 controls regioselectivity in other plant UGTs. 2. The L204G mutant shifted regioselectivity from C19 toward C13 on SG substrates. 3. This mutant suggests an alternative pathway for Reb E synthesis from Rub.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: L204A and L204W retained native regioselectivity but had strongly reduced catalytic activity, especially L204W
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates transferable engineering principle for redesigning UGT regioselectivity in SG biocatalysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a cross-species validation of the position-200 rule rather than a native Stevia pathway determinant.
